top_signals.title: Bohl: Iran Successfully Leveraged Hormuz In Negotiations
top_signals.summary:
President Trump signed an MOU to end the war in Iran & reopen the Strait of Hormuz after more than 100 days of the conflict. Ryan Bohl, Senior Middle East and North Africa Analyst at RANE spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the difference between this deal and the JCPOA under the Obama administration.

 (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Sprake: SARB Likely to Hike Before Year-End
top_signals.summary: Traders are paring back their bets on further interest-rate hikes by the South African Reserve Bank after inflation came in lower than expected. But Casey Sprake, Market Strategist at AG Capital told Bloomberg’s Chief Africa Correspondent Jennifer Zabasajja, they are expecting another rate hike before the end of the year. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
top_signals.summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Takaichi acknowledges proposed cut of consumption tax on food to 1%
top_signals.summary: The plan has divided party members and raised concerns about its impact on financial markets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
